campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review financial investments from March 2026, discuss a new policy limiting city property use for federal civil enforcement actions, and address current job openings and staffing challenges in the city.
keyTopics: March 2026 monthly investment report from Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority
New policy prohibiting use of city-owned property for federal civil enforcement
City job vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to review objections to cleaning up hazardous conditions on properties identified by the County, and voted to move forward with the cleanup work. The council also adopted a new technology policy for the city and approved a process for residents to request reasonable accommodations.
keyTopics: Hazardous property abatement and cleanup on county-identified properties
Public objections to the proposed abatement work
New City Technology Disruption Policy
Process for requesting reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on May 19, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the town's finances for March, and authorize several agreements and contracts. Key decisions included accepting a donation of emergency medical equipment, approving a five-year service agreement, moving forward with a paving project through O'Grady Paving, and applying for state grants.
keyTopics: Approved O'Grady Paving construction contract for road work
Accepted donation of Automated External Defibrillator (emergency medical equipment)
Reviewed town finances and investment report for March 2026
Authorized submission of grant applications for state funding
Adopted list of projects for Fiscal Year 2026-2027 budget

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 20, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to plan upcoming agendas and review committee work plans. The May 26 meeting was cancelled, so the council focused on organizing the June 2 agenda and updating plans for neighborhood services, education, and government operations.
keyTopics: Cancelled May 26 City Council meeting
June 2, 2026 draft agenda organization
Neighborhood Services and Education Committee work plan update
Rules and Open Government Committee work plan review
Council-sponsored events approval

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 20, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on May 20, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, hear public comments, and select members for the Youth Advisory Committee for the upcoming fiscal year. The council also reviewed agendas and reports from various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Youth Advisory Committee member selection for fiscal year 2026-27
Public comment period on city issues
City council meeting minutes approval
Board and commission agenda planning

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 19, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a regular meeting on May 19, 2026, to conduct city business and recognize two important observances. The council highlighted Asian Pacific American Heritage Month and Affordable Housing Month during the meeting.
keyTopics: Asian Pacific American Heritage Month recognition
Affordable Housing Month initiatives
April meeting minutes review

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 19, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to update the city's long-term health and safety plans through 2040, recognized the Public Works Department for their work on streets, parks, and infrastructure during Public Works Week, and celebrated Asian American Pacific Islander Month.
keyTopics: Updated Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (covering 2015-2040)
Public Works Department recognition for capital improvements, transportation, and environmental programs
Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) Month celebration
Some agenda items were postponed

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a special meeting of the Station Area Task Force on May 21, 2026, at City Hall to discuss local development and planning issues related to transit station areas. The meeting was open to the public both in person and online via Zoom.
keyTopics: Station Area Task Force special meeting convened
Public meeting held at City Hall (1500 Warburton Avenue)
In-person and virtual attendance options provided

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 19, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held several public hearings on May 19, 2026, to consider new city rules and approve development projects. Key decisions included appointing a permanent city manager and approving a site development permit amendment with a vesting tentative map for a property project.
keyTopics: New urgency ordinance and city code changes
Site development permit amendment and tentative map approval
Assessment confirmation and ordering (property-related)
Permanent city manager appointment

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council discussed efforts to help Palo Alto residents switch from gas to electric heating and appliances to meet air quality standards set by the regional Air District. The meeting included staff updates and proposals for programs that would support residents making these electrification upgrades.
keyTopics: Plans to help residents electrify homes (switch from gas to electric heating/appliances)
Compliance with Air District air quality regulations
Proposed principles and programs to support residential electrification
Environmental review status for electrification initiatives
